title: natural_features v1.2: Multiscale LLM Language Features
summary: Add an opt-in, provider-agnostic pipeline for high-quality language/semantic features from transcript or audio-derived transcript, sampled at multiple temporal scales (e.g., 2s, 4s, 16s) and returned as aligned feature matrices.
problem_statement: Current language features are baseline-oriented and mostly single-scale. Researchers need stronger semantic representations (LLM-grade) and ergonomic multiscale outputs for encoding/decoding analysis.
goals: Provide a one-call multiscale language feature workflow from transcript or audio.
Support opt-in API-backed embedding/LLM providers with secure key handling.
Return deterministic, time-aligned multiscale outputs as FeatureSeries and optional DataFrames.
Support sentence/paragraph-level semantic features in addition to token/word-level features.
Integrate with existing fMRI utilities (resampling, HRF, design matrix).
non_goals: Mandating any cloud provider for core installation.
Building a hosted orchestration service in this milestone.
target_users: Neuroimaging researchers requiring semantic regressors across multiple timescales.
NLP/ML researchers benchmarking semantic feature spaces for naturalistic stimuli.
Labs that need reproducible API-driven feature extraction with clear provenance.
assumptions: Users may have transcript text directly, or audio that can be transcribed to timestamped words.
Provider APIs can have latency/rate limits and occasional transient failures.
Users need strict reproducibility metadata even when embeddings come from remote APIs.

personas.name: Audio-only Researcher
needs: ASR fallback to generate timestamped transcript.
Same multiscale API regardless of transcript source.
Quality metadata to filter unreliable spans.

functional_requirements.name: Multiscale Feature Workflow API
functional_requirements.description: Implement a high-level API that extracts language features and returns outputs at user-specified scales.

api_contract.python: extract_multiscale_language(input, scales_s, feature_families, provider_config, as_dataframe=False, collapse=None) -> MultiscaleLanguageResult
api_contract.result_shape: dict[float, FeatureSeries] and optional dict[float, DataFrame]

functional_requirements.name: Input Modalities
functional_requirements.description: Support transcript input (with optional timestamps) and audio input (with ASR transcript fallback).

functional_requirements.name: LLM/Embedding Provider Abstraction
functional_requirements.description: Provide provider adapters for API-backed embeddings behind optional extras and configuration.

providers_initial: anthropic (opt-in, if embedding endpoint available in configured account)

functional_requirements.name: API Key and Credential Handling
functional_requirements.description: Load credentials from environment or explicit runtime config without persisting keys to artifacts, logs, or cache keys.

details.error_behavior: Fail fast with actionable message when provider selected but key missing.

functional_requirements.name: Semantic Unitization
functional_requirements.description: Generate semantic units at word, sentence, and paragraph/segment levels from timestamped text.

details.timestamp_policy: Each unit gets onset/offset with clear alignment policy (onset, center, or duration-weighted).

functional_requirements.name: Multiscale Rendering
functional_requirements.description: Render semantic unit features to multiple temporal grids in one pass.

functional_requirements.name: Feature Families
functional_requirements.description: Expose configurable feature families for language modeling.

families: lexical controls (length, frequency if lexicon available)

functional_requirements.name: Caching and Provenance for API Features
functional_requirements.description: Cache provider responses by text+model+params hash and store provenance metadata for reproducibility.

functional_requirements.name: fMRI Compatibility
functional_requirements.description: Outputs must be directly usable with existing `natural_features.fmri` transforms and remain compatible with `fmrimod` integration points.

functional_requirements.name: Recipe and CLI Support
functional_requirements.description: Allow declarative multiscale language extraction via recipe YAML and CLI flags.

non_functional_requirements.name: Security
non_functional_requirements.description: Never persist raw API keys in run logs, metadata, manifests, or cache artifacts.

non_functional_requirements.name: Reliability
non_functional_requirements.description: Provider calls implement retry with backoff and classify transient vs permanent errors.

non_functional_requirements.name: Determinism
non_functional_requirements.description: Given identical text/model/provider params and cached responses, outputs are bitwise stable.

non_functional_requirements.name: Performance
non_functional_requirements.description: Batch requests and chunk processing to control cost and latency for long transcripts.

non_functional_requirements.name: Ergonomics
non_functional_requirements.description: Single-call API and concise defaults for common scale sets (2, 4, 16 seconds).

feature_series_contract.times_s: monotonic, aligned to selected scale grid
feature_series_contract.coords.feature: stable names with provider/model prefixing where needed

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given valid input and `scales_s=[2,4,16]`, API returns three FeatureSeries with aligned time grids at exactly those scales.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Unit test checks keys, grid spacing, monotonic times, and non-empty matrices.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Audio-only input generates timestamped transcript events and then multiscale features without user-supplied transcript.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Integration test asserts ASR fallback path and output presence for all requested scales.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When provider API key is missing, system fails with actionable error; no secrets appear in logs/artifacts.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Negative test inspects exceptions and run logs for redaction.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Repeated run with same text/provider/model/params yields cache hit and identical outputs.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Integration test compares hashes and cache hit metrics.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Transient provider errors are retried with backoff; permanent errors fail fast with classified reason.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Mock provider tests for HTTP 429/5xx vs 4xx behavior.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Sentence and paragraph units are both renderable to multiscale grids with selectable alignment policy.
Outputs from each scale can be passed directly to `fmri.resample`, `fmri.hrf`, and `fmri.design.concat` without schema conversion.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Integration test exercises full transform chain.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Equivalent multiscale extraction is runnable from recipe and CLI with explicit provider config.
acceptance_criteria.verification: CLI test with fixture recipe and mocked provider.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Provider/model drift over time affects feature consistency.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Record provider/model metadata and optionally pin model revisions; encourage local cache snapshots.
Batching, caching, chunking, and configurable family selection.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Timestamp quality from ASR impacts semantic alignment.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Surface QC metrics and support optional alignment refinement.
Expose provider/model in feature names and metadata; discourage naive pooling across providers.

open_questions: Default semantic unit policy: sentence segmentation vs fixed-duration text chunks?
Whether to include provider-specific embedding dimensionality normalization by default.
Whether to support optional prompt-based latent dimensions (valence, sociality, etc.) in this milestone or v1.3.
